input.name: physics-artist
input.prompt:
You are a scientific ASCII artist. Produce two ASCII diagrams for *The Practitioner's Handbook of Extradimensional Navigation (3rd Edition)*. Return them in your final message to the team lead — do NOT write them to files.

## Standards
- Width: 60–80 characters per line (including borders)
- Wrap each diagram in a triple-backtick Markdown code block
- Put a plain-text title line ABOVE each code block
- Figure number and caption go INSIDE the code block, at the bottom
- Use box-drawing characters where appropriate: ─ │ ┌ ┐ └ ┘ ├ ┤ ┬ ┴ ┼ ╔ ╗ ╚ ╝ ║ ═
- Label ALL axes, curves, regions, and key features

---

## DIAGRAM D-1: Light Cones and Worldlines

A Minkowski spacetime diagram:
- ct (time) axis pointing UP, labeled at top
- x (space) axis pointing RIGHT, labeled at right
- Origin labeled O
- Two light-cone arms at EXACTLY ±45° from O, labeled 'light ray (c)' on both sides
- Region above the cone arms: 'future light cone'
- Region below the cone arms: 'past light cone'
- Three worldlines originating at O:
  1. A steep near-vertical line: 'timelike (observer)'
  2. A 45° line (same as right light ray): 'null (photon)' — annotate separately
  3. A dashed shallow line below 45°: 'spacelike (forbidden)'
- Caption at bottom: 'Fig. D-1: All physical worldlines must remain within the light cone.'

Spacetime diagram rules: ct axis is VERTICAL (up = future), x axis is HORIZONTAL. Light rays travel at exactly 45° (slope ±1 in ct-x space). Timelike worldlines are STEEPER than 45° (more vertical). Spacelike paths are SHALLOWER than 45° (more horizontal) — these are forbidden for matter.

---

## DIAGRAM D-2: Flat vs. Curved Spacetime

Two side-by-side panels fitting within ~78 characters total:

LEFT PANEL (~35 chars): Flat Minkowski space
- Evenly spaced vertical parallel worldlines (straight up)
- Horizontal grid lines (evenly spaced)
- Label: 'Flat spacetime'
- Label: '(special relativity)'

RIGHT PANEL (~35 chars): Curved spacetime near mass M
- A central point or marker labeled 'M' (the mass)
- Worldlines that curve/converge toward M
- Grid lines that pinch/curve near M
- Label: 'Curved spacetime'
- Label: '(near mass M)'

Separate the panels with a gap or vertical divider. Caption below both panels:
'Fig. D-2: Mass-energy curves spacetime, deflecting geodesics.'

When you are done, send your completed diagrams to the team lead (agent name: "team-lead").

description: physics-artist: You are a scientific ASCII artist. Produce two ASC...

content.text:
Spawned successfully.
agent_id: physics-artist@extradimensional-nav
name: physics-artist
team_name: extradimensional-nav
The agent is now running and will receive instructions via mailbox.

input.name: topology-artist
input.prompt:
You are a scientific ASCII artist specializing in abstract topology. Produce one ASCII diagram for *The Practitioner's Handbook of Extradimensional Navigation (3rd Edition)*. Return it in your final message to the team lead — do NOT write it to a file.

## Standards
- Width: 60–80 characters per line (including borders)
- Wrap the diagram in a triple-backtick Markdown code block
- Put a plain-text title line ABOVE the code block
- Figure number and caption go INSIDE the code block, at the bottom
- Use box-drawing characters for the outer border: ╔ ╗ ╚ ╝ ║ ═
- Use ASCII art characters (, ), |, /, \, ~, o, @, *, {, }, S, 8 etc. to suggest curves, loops, self-intersections

---

## DIAGRAM D-3: Calabi-Yau Cross-Section (Schematic)

A stylized 2D projection of a Calabi-Yau manifold — a complex, looping, self-intersecting shape suggesting 6D compactified topology. This is a SCHEMATIC — artistic impression only, not geometrically precise.

Requirements:
- Outer border using ╔ ╝ etc. box-drawing characters, ~70 chars wide
- The interior should be filled with an intricate ASCII art shape that:
  - Has multiple nested loops or figure-eight shapes
  - Has self-intersecting curves (lines that cross themselves)
  - Has regions of different "density" suggesting interior structure
  - Uses a variety of ASCII curve characters to suggest smooth curves: ~ ( ) / \ | * o @ 
  - Suggests topological complexity — like a pretzel or Klein bottle cross-section
  - Has a "hollow" feel with complex interior passages
- Label inside or near the shape: 'compactified dimensions (not to scale)'
- Label inside or near shape: 'actual scale: ~10^-32 m'
- The shape should fill most of the interior, roughly 15–20 lines tall
- Be CREATIVE and COMPLEX — this represents a 6-dimensional object projected to 2D

Caption at bottom (inside code block):
'Fig. D-3: Schematic cross-section of the Calabi-Yau fiber at each point'
'of 4D spacetime. The topology of this manifold determines the physical'
'constants of our brane.'

When you are done, send your completed diagram to the team lead (agent name: "team-lead").

description: topology-artist: You are a scientific ASCII artist specializing in ...

input.name: cosmo-artist
input.prompt:
You are a scientific ASCII artist. Produce two ASCII diagrams for *The Practitioner's Handbook of Extradimensional Navigation (3rd Edition)*. Return them in your final message to the team lead — do NOT write them to files.

## Standards
- Width: 60–80 characters per line (including borders)
- Wrap each diagram in a triple-backtick Markdown code block
- Put a plain-text title line ABOVE each code block
- Figure number and caption go INSIDE the code block, at the bottom
- Use box-drawing characters where appropriate: ─ │ ┌ ┐ └ ┘ ├ ┤ ┬ ┴ ┼ ╔ ╗ ╚ ╝ ║ ═
- Label ALL elements clearly

---

## DIAGRAM D-4: Brane Cosmology — Our Universe in the Bulk

A layered diagram showing nested structure:

OUTER CONTAINER: A large rectangle with double-line borders (╔ ╗ ╚ ╝ ║ ═), ~72 chars wide.
Label at top (inside or just below top border): '11-dimensional Bulk'

INSIDE the bulk, three horizontal bands separated by double lines (═══):

TOP BAND (~3 lines tall):
  - Label: 'Adjacent brane (dark matter signature)'
  - Single-line border top and bottom

MIDDLE BAND (~5 lines tall, thicker, visually emphasized — use ░░░ or === fill):
  - Label centered: 'Our universe (3-brane)'
  - Sub-label: '★ you are here'
  - This is the most important band — make it visually distinct (e.g., fill with light shading ░)

BOTTOM BAND (~3 lines tall):
  - Label: 'Adjacent brane (unobserved)'
  - Single-line border

BETWEEN BANDS: Vertical arrows pointing between bands (use ↑ ↓ or ^ v or │ with arrowheads), labeled:
  'gravity leaks through bulk'

Caption at bottom (inside code block):
'Fig. D-4: Our observable universe is a 3-brane embedded in the 11D bulk.'
'Only gravity (and Manifold-class vessels) crosses brane boundaries.'

---

## DIAGRAM D-5: Casimir Resonance Lattice (CRL) Schematic

An equipment cross-section diagram:

OUTER HOUSING: A large rectangle with DOUBLE WALLS — draw two concentric rectangles suggesting thick containment walls. Use ╔ ╗ ╚ ╝ ║ ═ for outer wall, ┌ ┐ └ ┘ │ ─ for inner wall. ~72 chars wide.

INTERIOR (between inner walls): 
- A repeating pattern of at least 3 vertical plate pairs. Each plate pair looks like:
    │▓│   │▓│
  Where │▓│ = a plate (thick vertical line or block), and the space between the pair = 'negative energy density zone'
- Label above the plate array: 'Casimir plate array (lambda-tuned)'
- Label between each plate pair (or with a bracket): 'neg. E zone'

LEFT SIDE (outside or at left edge of inner housing):
- An arrow pointing RIGHT (→) into the device
- Label: 'Power input (continuous)'

RIGHT SIDE (outside or at right edge of inner housing):
- An arrow pointing RIGHT (→) exiting the device  
- Label: 'Exotic matter output (metered)'

SAFETY WARNING: A small box inside the diagram (near bottom), using ┌─┐ └─┘ borders:
  ┌─ CAUTION ─────────────────────┐
  │ Lattice failure →             │
  │ rapid positive-energy inversion│
  └───────────────────────────────┘

Caption at bottom (inside code block):
'Fig. D-5: Cross-section of a Mark IV Casimir Resonance Lattice. Plate'
'separation is tunable to adjust exotic matter yield. Total output is'
'thermodynamically bounded.'

When you are done, send your completed diagrams to the team lead (agent name: "team-lead").
